subject: computer-science
description: Languages of Computer MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Computer languages are broadly classified into how many main levels?
mcqs.explanation: Computer languages are classified into three main levels: Low-Level (Machine), Middle-Level (Assembly) and High-Level.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is also known as Machine Language?
mcqs.explanation: The Low-Level Language is also called Machine Language. It uses 1s and 0s to create instructions.

mcqs.q: Machine language uses which of the following to create instructions?
mcqs.explanation: Machine language uses 1s and 0s (binary) — numeric codes that the computer can execute directly.

mcqs.q: Which type of language is also known as Assembly Language?
mcqs.explanation: Middle-Level Language is also called Assembly Language. It uses short mnemonic codes for instructions.

mcqs.q: Assembly Language uses which of the following to create instructions?
mcqs.explanation: Assembly Language uses short mnemonic codes (like MOV, ADD, SUB) instead of binary, making it easier to read than machine language.

mcqs.q: Which language is most similar to human language?
mcqs.explanation: High-Level Languages (e.g. COBOL, FORTRAN, BASIC, C, C++, Java) are designed to be similar to human language.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a High-Level Language?
mcqs.explanation: Assembly is a Middle-Level (Assembly) Language. COBOL, FORTRAN and Java are all High-Level Languages.

mcqs.q: Languages designed to express mathematical or symbolic computations are called:
mcqs.explanation: Algorithmic languages (such as FORTRAN and ALGOL) are designed to express mathematical or symbolic computations.

mcqs.q: Which type of languages help manage complexity in large programmes?
mcqs.explanation: Object-oriented languages (e.g. C++, Java, C#) help manage complexity in large programmes through encapsulation, inheritance and polymorphism.

mcqs.q: Declarative languages specify:
options: How a task is to be done
What is to be done rather than how
mcqs.explanation: Declarative languages (also called nonprocedural or very high-level) specify WHAT is to be done rather than HOW to do it.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is another name for declarative languages?
mcqs.explanation: Declarative languages are also called nonprocedural or very high-level languages.

mcqs.q: Scripting languages are sometimes called:
mcqs.explanation: Scripting languages are sometimes called 'little languages' because they solve relatively small programming problems without heavy data declarations.

mcqs.q: Which is the first important algorithmic language ever designed?
mcqs.explanation: FORTRAN (Formula Translation) was the first important algorithmic language — designed in 1957 by an IBM team led by John Backus.

mcqs.q: FORTRAN was designed in which year?
mcqs.explanation: FORTRAN was designed in 1957 by an IBM team led by John Backus.

mcqs.q: FORTRAN stands for:
mcqs.explanation: FORTRAN stands for Formula Translation — designed for mathematical and scientific computing.

mcqs.q: FORTRAN was developed at which company?
mcqs.explanation: FORTRAN was developed at IBM in 1957, led by John Backus.

mcqs.q: ALGOL stands for:
mcqs.explanation: ALGOL stands for Algorithmic Language — designed by American and European computer scientists during 1958–60.

mcqs.q: LISP was developed by John McCarthy around which year?
mcqs.explanation: LISP (List Processing) was developed about 1960 by John McCarthy at MIT, founded on the mathematical theory of recursive functions.

mcqs.q: LISP stands for:
mcqs.explanation: LISP stands for List Processing — developed by John McCarthy at MIT around 1960.

mcqs.q: LISP was developed at which institution?
mcqs.explanation: LISP was developed by John McCarthy at MIT (Massachusetts Institute of Technology) around 1960.

mcqs.q: COBOL was first introduced in which year?
mcqs.explanation: COBOL has been heavily used by businesses since its inception in 1959.

mcqs.q: COBOL stands for:
mcqs.explanation: COBOL stands for Common Business-Oriented Language.

mcqs.q: The C programming language was developed in:
mcqs.explanation: The C language was developed in 1972 by Dennis Ritchie and Brian Kernighan at AT&T Corporation.

mcqs.q: Who developed the C language?
mcqs.explanation: C was developed by Dennis Ritchie and Brian Kernighan at AT&T Corporation in 1972, mainly for OS programming.

mcqs.q: How many basic data types does the C language have?
mcqs.explanation: C has 5 basic data types: int, char, float, double and void.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a basic data type in C?
mcqs.explanation: C has 5 basic data types: int, char, float, double and void. 'string' is NOT a basic data type in C — strings are implemented as arrays of char.

mcqs.q: C++ was developed by:
mcqs.explanation: C++ was developed by Bjarne Stroustrup at AT&T in the mid-1980s — extending C by adding objects while preserving efficiency.

mcqs.q: C++ extended C by adding:
mcqs.explanation: C++ extended C by adding objects (object-oriented features) while preserving the efficiency of C.

mcqs.q: C# (C Sharp) was developed by Anders Hejlsberg at:
mcqs.explanation: C# was developed by Anders Hejlsberg at Microsoft in 2000.

mcqs.q: C# was released in which year?
mcqs.explanation: C# (pronounced 'C sharp') was developed by Anders Hejlsberg at Microsoft in 2000.

mcqs.q: Pascal was designed by Niklaus Wirth around which year?
mcqs.explanation: Pascal was designed about 1970 by Niklaus Wirth of Switzerland to teach structured programming.

mcqs.q: Pascal was designed primarily to teach:
mcqs.explanation: Pascal was designed by Niklaus Wirth to teach structured programming, emphasising conditional and loop control structures WITHOUT GOTO statements.

mcqs.q: BASIC stands for:
mcqs.explanation: BASIC stands for Beginners All-purpose Symbolic Instruction Code — designed at Dartmouth in the mid-1960s by John Kemeny and Thomas Kurtz.

mcqs.q: BASIC was designed at:
mcqs.explanation: BASIC was designed at Dartmouth College in the mid-1960s by John Kemeny and Thomas Kurtz.

mcqs.q: Logo originated as a simplified dialect of which language?
mcqs.explanation: Logo originated in the late 1960s as a simplified LISP dialect for education.

mcqs.q: Hypertalk was designed by Bill Atkinson for:
mcqs.explanation: Hypertalk was designed as 'programming for the rest of us' by Bill Atkinson for Apple's Macintosh.

mcqs.q: Ada was developed in the early 1980s for:
mcqs.explanation: Ada was developed in the early 1980s for the US Department of Defense for large-scale programming.

mcqs.q: Java was designed by which company?
mcqs.explanation: Java was designed in the early 1990s by Sun Microsystems, Inc. as a programming language for the World Wide Web (WWW).

mcqs.q: Java was originally designed as a programming language for:
mcqs.explanation: Java was designed in the early 1990s by Sun Microsystems as a programming language for the World Wide Web (WWW).

mcqs.q: Visual Basic was developed by Microsoft to extend:
mcqs.explanation: Visual Basic was developed by Microsoft to extend the capabilities of BASIC by adding objects and 'event-driven' programming.

mcqs.q: Python was developed in which year?
mcqs.explanation: Python is an open-source language developed by Dutch programmer Guido van Rossum in 1991.

mcqs.q: Who developed the Python programming language?
mcqs.explanation: Python was developed by Dutch programmer Guido van Rossum in 1991.

mcqs.q: Python uses which of the following to group statements?
mcqs.explanation: Python is unique in that it uses indentation (whitespace) instead of brackets/braces to group statements.

mcqs.q: PROLOG stands for:
mcqs.explanation: PROLOG stands for Programming in Logic. It states a programme as a set of logical relations.

mcqs.q: PROLOG states a programme as:
mcqs.explanation: PROLOG states a programme as a set of logical relations — making it ideal for AI and theorem-proving applications.

mcqs.q: LISP, ML and Haskell are typically used as:
options: Research tools, automated theorem provers and some commercial projects
mcqs.explanation: LISP, ML and Haskell are used as research tools in language development, automated mathematical theorem provers, and some commercial projects.

mcqs.q: Pascal was designed by Niklaus Wirth in which country?
mcqs.explanation: Pascal was designed about 1970 by Niklaus Wirth of Switzerland.

mcqs.q: FORTRAN was led by which IBM scientist?
mcqs.explanation: FORTRAN was designed in 1957 by an IBM team led by John Backus.

mcqs.q: SQL stands for:
mcqs.explanation: SQL stands for Structured Query Language — used for specifying the organisation of databases (collections of records).

mcqs.q: SQL is a language for:
mcqs.explanation: SQL is a language for specifying the organisation of databases — collections of records.

mcqs.q: TeX was developed during 1977–86 by:
mcqs.explanation: TeX was developed during 1977–86 as a text-formatting language by Donald Knuth, a Stanford University professor, to improve mathematical notation in his books.

mcqs.q: PostScript is a:
mcqs.explanation: PostScript is a page-description language developed in the early 1980s by Adobe Systems Incorporated, based on work at Xerox PARC.

mcqs.q: PostScript was developed by:
mcqs.explanation: PostScript was developed in the early 1980s by Adobe Systems Incorporated.

mcqs.q: SGML stands for:
mcqs.explanation: SGML stands for Standard Generalized Markup Language — an international standard for the definition of markup languages.

mcqs.q: HTML stands for:
mcqs.explanation: HTML stands for Hypertext Markup Language — the markup language for encoding Web pages.

mcqs.q: XML is a simplified form of which language?
mcqs.explanation: XML (Extensible Markup Language) is a simplified form of SGML, intended for documents published on the Web.

mcqs.q: CGI stands for:
mcqs.explanation: CGI stands for Common Gateway Interface — it transmits requests and responses between the reader's Web browser and the Web server that provides the page.
